item.title: Nvidia’s Huang Tries to Get Investors to See the Future of AI
item.description: The chipmaker’s CEO argues another big wave of AI demand is coming

item.title: ICE Raids Did Lasting Damage to American Businesses
item.description: In one corner of Charlotte, foot traffic and sales remain depressed six months after deportation raids.

item.title: The AI Boom Is Helping Asia Exporters Weather the Energy Shock
item.description: Chip exports from South Korea and Japan are surging, helping offset headwinds from high fuel costs

item.title: EU to Seek Carve-Out for Banned China Chips to Shield Auto Firms
item.description: The European Union will propose temporarily lifting sanctions on a Chinese semiconductor supplier after automakers warned of impending supply chain chaos if the ban isn’t removed.

item.title: Hungarian Budget Data Was ‘Falsified,’ Premier Magyar Warns
item.description: Hungary will probably have a “very high” budget shortfall this year, Prime Minister Peter Magyar said, adding that Viktor Orban’s administration had likely “falsified” data.

item.title: Vietnam’s Convicted Mogul Repays $455 Million to Bondholders
item.description: Vietnam real estate tycoon Truong My Lan, convicted in the nation’s largest fraud case, has repaid a total of more than 12 trillion dong ($455 million) to about 42,000 bond holders as the government works to clawback billions of ill-gotten gains.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Could Be Just the Start of the Mega Listings
item.description: OpenAI, Anthropic are gearing up for listings later this year.

item.title: Stockholm Index Briefly Halted as New Glitch Hits Nasdaq Nordics
item.description: Stockholm’s blue-chip share index was disrupted by a technical glitch on Thursday, the second time in less than a month that Nasdaq Inc. has suffered problems with its Nordic benchmarks.

item.title: Ford Is the Latest Old Economy Company Caught Up in the AI Boom
item.description: AI is giving some old-school manufacturers a profit boost.

item.title: Deere Gets Lift From Construction as Farmers Still Struggle
item.description: Deere & Co. is getting a boost from its construction and forestry segments, helping to offset volatile agriculture markets that continue to crimp farmer spending.

item.title: UK Must Harness Flexible Grids Before Upgrades to Curb Bills
item.description: The UK’s energy regulator said distribution grids must use flexibility tools such as smart technology to better control demand before being allowed to make network investments that add to customers’ bills.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Requires Leap of Faith in AI, Mars and Musk’s Vision
item.description: Elon Musk’s SpaceX pulled back the curtain on a business empire that has racked up ballooning losses and debt after acquiring a cash-hungry startup, and pumping billions of dollars into futuristic endeavors ranging from AI to a Mars rocket.

item.title: Quadria-Backed Aragen Life Said to Plan $300 Million India IPO
item.description: Aragen Life Sciences Ltd., a contract research and manufacturing firm, is considering an initial public offering in Mumbai that could raise as much as $300 million, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Investec Sweetens Private-Banking Offering to Lure Rich Clients
item.description: Investec is stepping up its private-banking offering in South Africa and in the UK as the lender looks to attract rich clients across the world and position itself for further growth.

item.title: Codelco Fires Executive for Overstating Copper Output in Chile
item.description: Codelco fired one executive and reprimanded several others after an internal audit found Chile’s state copper producer overstated part of its 2025 output by classifying material that needed further processing as finished product.

item.title: War Weighs on Global Growth With Inflation Worries Intensifying
item.description: The global economy is showing signs of waning momentum and mounting inflation pressures during its third month of a war-induced energy crunch.

item.title: Euro-Zone Growth Is Buckling Under Weight of War Impact
item.description: The euro area will slow markedly while suffering the fastest inflation since 2023 as it succumbs to the energy-cost surge from the Iran war, according to the European Commission.

item.title: Streeting Backs Hiking UK Capital Gains Levy to Match Income Tax
item.description: UK leadership hopeful Wes Streeting has come out in favor of equalizing the capital gains and income tax rates, in the latest sign of broadening support in the ruling Labour party for a massive increase in levies on investments by the highest-earners.

item.title: Indonesian Palm Oil Sinks as Commodity Overhaul Sows Confusion
item.description: Indonesian palm oil prices tumbled after President Prabowo Subianto unveiled plans to impose state control over exports of several key commodities, including the edible oil.

item.title: A Once-Empty Hotel Is Now a Hub for Dealmakers Rushing to Venezuela
item.description: For most of the 13 years the Cayena hotel has operated in Caracas, rooms starting at $400 a night mostly sat empty. The occupancy rate was a dismal 21% last year.

item.title: Microsoft’s Sliding Stock, AI Woes Make It Market’s Biggest Drag
item.description: The S&P 500 Index has had a fitful 2026 as it aims for a fourth-straight year of double-digit percentage gains, something it hasn’t done since the 1990s, while contending with risks from the war in Iran and rising inflation.

item.title: German Weapons Maker Rheinmetall Selling First Bond in 16 Years
item.description: Rheinmetall AG has begun selling €500 million ($580 million) of new debt in its first public bond offering since 2010.

item.title: Manus Weighs Raising $1 Billion to Unwind Meta Takeover
item.description: The co-founders of Manus are exploring options to fulfill Beijing’s demand to unwind a controversial takeover by Meta Platforms Inc., including raising about $1 billion from external investors to buy back the Chinese-founded AI operation.

item.title: UK Private Sector Shrinks as Starmer Crisis Adds to Iran Shock
item.description: British businesses posted the first decline in output in over a year as the Iran shock and a mounting rebellion against Prime Minister Keir Starmer hit activity in the services sector, a survey showed.

item.title: Morocco Backs Gaming Firms in Push to Spawn First ‘Unicorns’
item.description: Morocco will provide financial backing to local gaming startups, part of a state-sponsored push to generate more skilled jobs in the creative industries, diversify the economy and boost export revenue.

item.title: Samsung Chip Workers to Get Average $340,000 Bonus in AI Boom
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co. could distribute about 40 trillion won ($26.6 billion) to chip employees as a bonus for this year after the company struck a last-minute deal with labor unions to avert a strike.

item.title: China Warns EU of Retaliation as Bloc Mulls Tougher Trade Curbs
item.description: China will take countermeasures if the European Union presses ahead with new restrictions on Chinese imports, a Ministry of Commerce official said, as trade tensions between the two blocs continue to rise.

item.title: Iran Reviewing Trump’s Latest Offer as Clock Ticks on Ceasefire
item.description: Iran is assessing the latest peace proposal from the US, as the countries seek to avoid a resumption of hostilities.

item.title: Why Cerebras CEO Andrew Feldman Built The World’s Largest Computer Chip
item.description: Bigger means faster in the world of AI chipmaking.

item.title: Odd Lots: Why Cerebras Built The Largest Computer Chip (Podcast)
item.description: Size is the name of the game for the AI chipmaker Cerebras: Their chips are truly massive, about the size of a dinner plate. According to Andrew Feldman, CEO and founder of Cerebras, that is about 58 times larger than the average chip. That sheer size enables blazing fast inference for AI queries. Feldman joins us on the week of his company’s IPO to talk about his core product and how it fits into the AI boom. We discuss the history of the GPU, competition between open-and closed-source models,
